ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-19-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Justin Macneil (Trustee) in Propria Persona.

The Court directed Ms. Macneil to properly title amendments in the future. Ms. Macneil addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes with regard to $1,657.50 having been paid and the calculation of the interest on the pecuniary gift. The Court granted the Third Amended 4th and Final Account and Report with the deleniation of $1,657.50 and the correction of the interest amount on the gift (subject to review/modification of the order).

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (132 25th Avenue, San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Nov-29-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Justin McNeil (Trustee/Petitioner) in Propria Persona.

The sale was confirmed at $1,000,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order). All of the proceeds from the sale including the $100,000.00 depsosit which is in escrow are to be deposited into a blocked account. Status of filing of the receipt for the blocked account was set for December 27, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: J. Chien.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-15-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Justin Macneil in Propria Persona.

Ms. Macneil stated that Ms. Magnussen had moved out of the house, informed the Court of Ms. Magnussen's passing and stated that the next of kin refused the get involved. In addition, Ms. Macneil made an oral request to be given authority to use Trust funds to pay for burial expenses, authority to communicate with the Medical Examiner, stated that there was no reason to not proceed with the sale of the house and that she could enter into a listing agreement by the end of March. The Court directed Ms. Macneil to file an ex parte petition with regard to the oral requests. The Court granted the Petition for Instructions (subject to review of the written order).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-08-2010 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Feb-08-2010 Continued To Mar-15-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Justin Macneil in Propria Persona.

Ms. Magnussen was not present. Ms. Macneil stated that the issue regarding vacating of the property was moot as the Beneficiary had signed a rental agreement for an apartment, and that the property was to be vacated by March 1, 2010. The matter was continued to March 15, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jun-03-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Justin McNeil in Pro Per.

Ms. McNeil stated that she had tried everything she could to help the beneficiary and that the beneficiary was upset. The Court granted the petition, (signing the Order Re Instructions For Disposition Of Real Property in open court).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Peppina Thompson CSR#7433. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT
Off Calendar Oct-04-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Justin McNeil appearing as successor trustee.
The Court questioned how the loan on the property is being paid and what will happen when the funds run out. Ms. McNeil reported that the loan is being paid by the proceeds of the loan, that the trust does not have any other income and that the purpose of the loan was to make repairs on the porperty. Ms. McNeil also stated that she would have to seek private financing in the future and that she would defer payment of the fees. Matter submitted.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Bailiff: O. Arias Clerk: Rosie Noguera

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Jun-05-2007 Continued To Jul-24-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Warren Sullivan (Specially appearing for Justin W. McNeil, Trustee).

The Court noted at the outset that there had already been a number of continuances for the filing of the accounting and impressed upon Mr. Sullivan the importance of the filing of an accounting. Mr. Sullivan reported that Mr. McNeil was out of the country and he would convey the Court's concerns to Mr. McNeil upon his return. That said, the Court continued the matter to July 24, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 with the admonishment that this continuance would be the last.

Judicial Officer: John Dearman, Judge; Court Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR# 11722; Bailiff: Deputy Larry Thompson; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Tim Kneis.
